query: 2026 Venezuela earthquakes June 24 confirmed death toll latest figures, trajectory of confirmed deaths reported by authorities and Wikipedia, number of missing persons and rescue operation status
answer:
I've completed the research on the June 24, 2026 Venezuela earthquakes. Here's a summary of the key findings:

## Death Toll Trajectory (Authorities & Wikipedia)
The confirmed toll rose steeply over the first week:
- **June 24–25:** 188 dead
- **June 25:** ~235 dead
- **June 29:** 1,719 dead (UN/Venezuelan authorities)
- **July 1:** ~2,295 dead
- **July 2:** **2,595 dead** (Acting President Delcy Rodríguez)
- **Wikipedia (current):** "more than 2,590 killed" — synced to the latest official count

## Missing Persons
- **~50,000 people unaccounted for** as of July 2, 2026
- Wikipedia describes "tens of thousands" missing

## Rescue Operations
- 2,000+ workers from 27 countries, 160+ dogs, 40+ teams deployed
- Teams worked past the 72-hour window; a 3-year-old was rescued alive after 6 days
- UN warned the toll would keep climbing and procured 10,000 body bags; operations transitioning toward recovery

## Key Caveats
- Latest confirmed figures date to **July 2** (2,595 deaths), so counts are likely still rising as of today (July 3)
- USGS PAGER *projected* a potential toll exceeding 100,000 — a model estimate, not a confirmed count
- Minor source variance exists (2,295 vs. 2,595) reflecting different reporting cutoffs in a fast-evolving situation
- The Wikipedia figure closely tracks the most recent official authority count

Full structured findings with 17 data points and 8 sources have been submitted.

sources.title: 2026 Venezuela earthquakes - Wikipedia
sources.snippet: More than 2,590 people were killed, over 12,500 were injured, and tens of thousands were reported missing. USGS PAGER predicted the death toll could exceed 100,000.

sources.title: Venezuela earthquakes latest: Death toll climbs as rescuers continue searching - ABC News
sources.snippet: At least 2,595 dead, 12,400 injured, nearly 50,000 unaccounted for as of July 2, 2026, per Acting President Delcy Rodriguez; over 862 aftershocks.

sources.title: Venezuela earthquake death toll passes 1,700 as UN continues to scale up response | UN News
sources.snippet: At least 1,719 confirmed dead as of Monday June 29; 2,000+ rescue workers from 27 countries, 160+ dogs, 40+ teams; missing figure not yet confirmed.

sources.title: Venezuela Earthquake Update: Magnitude 7.5 and 7.2 Quakes, Latest Figures, and Damage Areas - Miyamoto International
sources.snippet: As of July 1: ~2,295 deaths, 11,200+ injured; ~58,870 buildings damaged; ~$6.7B economic losses; UN procured 10,000 body bags.

sources.title: Untold casualties and humanitarian needs: What to know a week from Venezuela's quakes - NPR
sources.snippet: One week on, death toll ~2,295; 3-year-old rescued alive after 6 days; UN warns toll will keep rising; up to 6.8 million may need aid.

sources.title: Venezuela earthquakes kill at least 188 people, injure 1,520 - Al Jazeera
sources.snippet: At least 188 killed and 1,520 injured; at least 200 trapped; USGS casualty range 10,000–100,000.

sources.title: June 24-25, 2026 — Venezuela rocked by 7.5 and 7.2 magnitude earthquakes | CNN
sources.snippet: Death toll rose to around 235 with 4,300 injured, per health minister.

sources.title: Venezuela Earthquakes: Children Need Help Now | UNICEF USA
sources.snippet: UNICEF estimates 1.8 million people, including 680,000 children, need humanitarian assistance.

key_facts: Wikipedia (2026 Venezuela earthquakes) states 'more than 2,590 people were killed, over 12,500 were injured, and tens of thousands were reported missing.'
Latest authority figure: 2,595 confirmed deaths and 12,400 injured as of July 2, 2026, per Acting President Delcy Rodríguez (ABC News).
Nearly 50,000 people remain unaccounted for/missing as of July 2, 2026 (Venezuelan officials).
Two earthquakes struck June 24, 2026 at 18:04 VET: Mw 7.2 foreshock then Mw 7.5 mainshock ~39 seconds later; epicenters in Veroes Municipality, Yaracuy.
USGS PAGER projected the eventual toll could significantly exceed the confirmed count, potentially over 100,000 (range 10,000–100,000) — a model estimate, not confirmed.
Rescue operation (as of June 29): 2,000+ workers from 27 countries, 160+ search dogs, 40+ teams; continued past the 72-hour window as survivors were still being found.
A 3-year-old boy was rescued alive six days after the quakes (reported ~July 1).
UN procured 10,000 body bags; UN coordinator warned the death toll 'will unavoidably and sadly keep on growing.'

~58,870 buildings damaged/destroyed; economic losses estimated ~$6.7 billion; up to 6.8 million may need humanitarian assistance; UNICEF estimates 1.8 million people (incl. 680,000 children) need aid.
Over 862 aftershocks recorded following the June 24 quakes; strongest quake in Venezuela since the 1900 San Narciso earthquake.
Source discrepancy: Miyamoto/NPR cite ~2,295 deaths (July 1) while ABC/Wikipedia cite ~2,595 (July 2), reflecting differing reporting cutoffs.

data_points.metric: Confirmed deaths (latest authority, Acting President Delcy Rodríguez)
USGS PAGER projected potential death toll (upper bound)
